text:
Nisha Gopalan is a former Senior Overnight Assignment Editor for Investopedia News. She is an award-winning financial journalist who has worked in London, where she is currently based, and Hong Kong. She previously worked at The Wall Street Journal and Bloomberg.

Anadolu / Contributor / Getty Images



Arson attacks on France’s high-speed trains network sabotaged services just hours before the Paris Olympics were set to start Friday.

Eurostar, the train service under the English Channel, said its high-speed line is being diverted, extending journey times, and several trains are being canceled.The French state railway company SNCF said on its website that traffic is "disrupted" on its Atlantic, North, and East high-speed lines.

"Coordinated malicious acts targeted several TGV lines last night and will seriously disrupt traffic until this weekend," French Transport Minister Patrice Vergriete wrote on a post on the X social media network.

French Prime Minister Gabriel Attal said in a post on X that the attacks were carried out in a "prepared and coordinated manner," adding that "the consequences on the rail network are massive and serious."

According toThe New York Times, more than 1 million people were expected to travel on France's rail network this weekend. The French government has yet to release a statement saying who is responsible for the attacks.

Eurostar. “French railways network attack.”

Sncfconnect. “Traffic disrupted on the Atlantic, North and East high-speed lines.”

X Platform. “@P_Vergriete, 00:56 a.m. July 26, 2024.”

X Platform. “@GabrielAttal, 03:50 a.m. July 26, 2024.”

The New York Times. "Olympics Live Updates: Arson Attack Incites Travel Chaos Ahead of Opening Ceremony."

title: Sabotage Hits French Trains Hours Before Paris Olympics Begin
description: Arson attacks on France’s high-speed trains network sabotaged services just hours before the Paris Olympics were set to start Friday.
